test_results.goal: I want to become a full-stack web developer

matched_occupation.label: web developer
matched_occupation.description: Web developers develop, implement and document web-accessible software based on the designs provided. They align the client's web presence with its business strategy, troubleshoot software problems and issues and look for ways to improve the application.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Programming with Python
objectives: Understand basic programming concepts like variables, data types, and operators.
Learn to write simple programs using Python syntax.
Understand control flow statements (if/else, loops).
Learn to define and use functions.

learning_path.title: Web Programming Fundamentals: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
objectives: Understand the structure of a web page using HTML.
Learn to style web pages using CSS.
Learn to add interactivity to web pages using JavaScript.
Understand the client-side web development process.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Understand the purpose and benefits of using an IDE.
Learn to navigate and use a specific IDE (e.g., VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA).
Learn to debug code within the IDE.
Understand code completion and refactoring features.
skills: IDE usage (VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA, etc.)

learning_path.title: Web-Based Collaborative Platforms: Git and GitHub
objectives: Understand the concepts of version control.
Learn to use Git for tracking changes to code.
Learn to collaborate on code using GitHub.
Understand branching, merging, and pull requests.

learning_path.title: Tools for Software Configuration Management: Docker
objectives: Understand the concept of containerization.
Learn to create and manage Docker containers.
Understand Dockerfiles and Docker Compose.
Learn to deploy applications using Docker.

learning_path.title: Content Development Processes: Markdown and Static Site Generators
objectives: Learn to write content using Markdown.
Understand the benefits of static site generators.
Learn to use a static site generator (e.g., Jekyll, Hugo).
Understand how to deploy a static website.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Web Services: REST APIs
objectives: Understand the concept of web services.
Learn about RESTful APIs.
Learn to make API requests using tools like Postman or curl.
Understand API documentation and best practices.

learning_path.title: Using Application-Specific Interfaces: Example - Database Interaction with Python
objectives: Understand how to connect to a database from a Python application.
Learn to perform CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on a database.
Understand SQL queries.
Learn to handle database errors.

learning_path.title: Using Markup Languages: XML and JSON
objectives: Understand the purpose of markup languages.
Learn to write and parse XML documents.
Learn to write and parse JSON documents.
Understand the differences between XML and JSON.

learning_path.title: Using E-Services: Integrating with Payment Gateways
objectives: Understand the concept of e-services.
Learn about different payment gateways (e.g., Stripe, PayPal).
Learn to integrate with a payment gateway using its API.
Understand security considerations for e-commerce.

test_results.goal: I want to become a data scientist with machine learning expertise

matched_occupation.label: data engineer
matched_occupation.description: Data engineers develop the architecture needed to process, manage, and store large amounts of data which will be used by data scientists for analysis. They design the infrastructure and maintain data pipelines and warehouses to leverage data for strategic advantage.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Data Warehousing
objectives: Understand the fundamental concepts of data warehousing.
Differentiate between OLTP and OLAP systems.
Identify the key components of a data warehouse architecture.
Learn about different data warehouse modeling techniques (e.g., star schema, snowflake schema).

learning_path.title: Computer Science Fundamentals for Data Professionals
objectives: Understand basic data structures (arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs).
Learn about algorithms and their complexity (Big O notation).
Grasp the principles of operating systems and networking.
Understand the basics of programming paradigms (e.g., procedural, object-oriented).

learning_path.title: Data Storage Technologies: A Comprehensive Overview
objectives: Explore different data storage options (e.g., relational databases, NoSQL databases, cloud storage).
Understand the trade-offs between different storage technologies (e.g., cost, performance, scalability).
Learn about data storage formats (e.g., CSV, JSON, Parquet, ORC).
Understand the concepts of data backup and recovery.

learning_path.title: Working with Unstructured Data
objectives: Define unstructured data and its characteristics.
Learn about techniques for extracting information from unstructured data (e.g., text mining, image recognition).
Explore different tools and technologies for processing unstructured data (e.g., NLP libraries, computer vision frameworks).
Understand the challenges of storing and managing unstructured data.

learning_path.title: Managing Research Data: Best Practices and Tools
objectives: Understand the importance of research data management.
Learn about data management planning (DMP).
Explore best practices for data documentation, metadata creation, and data preservation.
Discover tools and resources for managing research data (e.g., data repositories, version control systems).

learning_path.title: Data Processing Techniques: ETL and Beyond
objectives: Understand the ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) process.
Learn about different data transformation techniques (e.g., data cleaning, data integration, data aggregation).
Explore modern data processing frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Apache Flink).
Understand the concepts of data pipelines and data orchestration.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of databases and SQL

learning_path.title: Establishing Data Processes: From Collection to Analysis
objectives: Learn how to define and document data processes.
Understand the importance of data quality and data validation.
Explore different data governance frameworks.
Learn how to monitor and improve data processes.

learning_path.title: Managing ICT Data Architecture: Principles and Practices
objectives: Understand the principles of ICT data architecture.
Learn about different data architecture patterns (e.g., data lake, data mesh).
Explore the role of data architects in an organization.
Understand the importance of data security and data privacy.
prerequisites: Experience with data warehousing and data management

learning_path.title: Storing Digital Data and Systems: Infrastructure Considerations
objectives: Understand different infrastructure options for storing digital data (e.g., on-premise, cloud, hybrid).
Learn about storage capacity planning and performance optimization.
Explore different storage technologies (e.g., SAN, NAS, object storage).
Understand the importance of data security and disaster recovery.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of computer networks and operating systems

learning_path.title: Creating Data Sets: From Raw Data to Analysis-Ready Formats
objectives: Learn how to identify and collect relevant data sources.
Understand data cleaning and data transformation techniques.
Explore different data set formats (e.g., CSV, JSON, Parquet).
Learn how to create data dictionaries and metadata documentation.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of data and databases

test_results.goal: I want to become an AI engineer specializing in deep learning

matched_occupation.label: artificial intelligence engineer
matched_occupation.description: Artificial intelligence engineer apply methods of artificial intelligence in engineering, robotics and computer science to design programs which simulate intelligence including thinking models, cognitive and knowledge-based systems, problem solving, and decision making. They also integrate structured knowledge into computer systems (ontologies, knowledge bases) in order to solve complex problems normally requiring a high level of human expertise or artificial intelligence methods.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Computer Simulation
objectives: Understand the fundamental concepts of computer simulation.
Learn different types of simulation (e.g., Monte Carlo, Discrete Event).
Identify real-world applications of computer simulation.
Gain hands-on experience with a basic simulation tool.

learning_path.title: Data Visualization Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the principles of effective data visualization.
Learn different types of charts and graphs and when to use them.
Explore data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Python libraries).
Create compelling visualizations to communicate insights from data.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of data and spreadsheets

learning_path.title: Algorithms: The Building Blocks of Computation
objectives: Understand the concept of an algorithm and its importance.
Learn common algorithmic techniques (e.g., searching, sorting).
Analyze the efficiency of algorithms (Big O notation).
Implement basic algorithms in a programming language.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks
objectives: Understand the basic structure and function of artificial neural networks.
Learn about different types of neural networks (e.g., feedforward, convolutional).
Train a simple neural network using a machine learning library (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch).
Apply neural networks to solve a simple classification or regression problem.
prerequisites: Understanding of linear algebra and calculus (optional but helpful)

learning_path.title: Information Extraction from Text
objectives: Understand the challenges of extracting information from unstructured text.
Learn techniques for named entity recognition (NER).
Explore methods for relationship extraction.
Use natural language processing (NLP) libraries (e.g., spaCy, NLTK) to extract information.

learning_path.title: Task Algorithmisation: Designing Efficient Processes
objectives: Learn how to break down complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps.
Understand different algorithm design paradigms (e.g., divide and conquer, greedy).
Develop algorithms for specific tasks.
Evaluate the efficiency and correctness of algorithms.

learning_path.title: Information Categorization and Classification
objectives: Understand the principles of information categorization.
Learn different classification techniques (e.g., rule-based, machine learning).
Evaluate the performance of classification models.
Apply classification techniques to real-world datasets.

learning_path.title: Working with Unstructured Data
objectives: Understand the characteristics of unstructured data.
Learn techniques for cleaning and pre-processing unstructured data.
Explore methods for extracting features from unstructured data.
Apply machine learning techniques to unstructured data.

learning_path.title: Principles of Artificial Intelligence
objectives: Understand the core concepts and history of AI.
Learn about different AI paradigms (e.g., symbolic AI, machine learning).
Explore ethical considerations in AI development.
Discuss the future of AI and its impact on society.

learning_path.title: Creatively Using Digital Technologies
objectives: Explore a range of digital technologies and their potential applications.
Learn techniques for brainstorming and ideation using digital tools.
Develop creative projects using digital technologies.
Present and critique creative digital projects.

test_results.goal: I want to become a DevOps engineer with cloud expertise

matched_occupation.label: cloud DevOps engineer
matched_occupation.description: Cloud DevOps engineers implement and manage continuous software delivery systems and methodologies. This includes managing and configuring code repositories, build services, automated testing, and deployment mechanisms. For cloud-based workloads, a Cloud DevOps Engineer define and deploy infrastructure as code, automating test and development environments. They can define and configure automated disaster recovery solutions that meet business objectives.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Software Frameworks
objectives: Understand the concept of a software framework.
Identify different types of software frameworks (e.g., web, mobile, desktop).
Learn the benefits of using software frameworks (e.g., code reuse, faster development).
Explore popular software frameworks (e.g., React, Angular, Spring).
skills: Conceptual understanding of software development principles

learning_path.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
objectives: Understand JavaScript syntax and data types.
Learn about variables, operators, and control flow.
Explore functions and objects in JavaScript.
Understand the Document Object Model (DOM) and how to manipulate it.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Computer Engineering
objectives: Understand the basic components of a computer system (CPU, memory, I/O).
Learn about computer architecture and organization.
Explore digital logic and circuits.
Understand the role of operating systems.

learning_path.title: Cloud Monitoring and Reporting Basics
objectives: Understand the importance of cloud monitoring.
Identify key metrics to monitor in a cloud environment (e.g., CPU utilization, memory usage, network traffic).
Learn about different cloud monitoring tools and services (e.g., CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Google Cloud Monitoring).
Understand how to create basic dashboards and reports.
skills: Basic understanding of cloud computing concepts

learning_path.title: Introduction to Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Understand the purpose of an IDE.
Explore the features of popular IDEs (e.g., VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse).
Learn how to use an IDE for code editing, debugging, and testing.
Understand how to configure an IDE for different programming languages.
skills: Basic programming experience in at least one language

learning_path.title: ICT System Programming Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the role of system programming in ICT.
Learn about system calls and APIs.
Explore low-level programming concepts (e.g., memory management, process management).
Understand the basics of operating system kernels.
skills: Strong programming skills in C or C++

learning_path.title: Jenkins: Introduction to Software Configuration Management
objectives: Understand the principles of Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD).
Learn how to install and configure Jenkins.
Explore Jenkins pipelines and jobs.
Understand how to integrate Jenkins with version control systems (e.g., Git).
skills: Basic understanding of software development lifecycle
Familiarity with version control systems (e.g., Git)

learning_path.title: Designing Cloud Architectures: Core Principles
objectives: Understand the key principles of cloud architecture (e.g., scalability, reliability, security, cost optimization).
Learn about different cloud deployment models (e.g., IaaS, PaaS, SaaS).
Explore common cloud architectural patterns (e.g., microservices, serverless).
Understand how to choose the right cloud services for a given application.
skills: Solid understanding of cloud computing concepts
Experience with software development and deployment

learning_path.title: Cloud Refactoring: Improving Existing Cloud Applications
objectives: Understand the concept of cloud refactoring.
Identify common problems in existing cloud applications (e.g., performance bottlenecks, security vulnerabilities, cost inefficiencies).
Learn different cloud refactoring techniques (e.g., migrating to serverless, optimizing database queries, improving caching).
Understand how to measure the impact of cloud refactoring efforts.
skills: Experience with cloud application development and deployment

learning_path.title: Designing Cloud Networks: Security and Performance
objectives: Understand the fundamentals of cloud networking (e.g., VPCs, subnets, routing).
Learn how to design secure cloud networks using firewalls, network security groups, and VPNs.
Explore techniques for optimizing cloud network performance (e.g., load balancing, content delivery networks).
Understand how to monitor and troubleshoot cloud network issues.
skills: Solid understanding of networking concepts (e.g., TCP/IP, DNS)

test_results.goal: I want to become a mobile app developer for iOS and Android

matched_occupation.label: mobile application developer
matched_occupation.description: Mobile application developers implement applications software for mobile devices, based on the designs provided, using general or specific development tools for device operating systems.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Python Programming
objectives: Understand the basic syntax of Python.
Learn about data types (integers, floats, strings, booleans).
Write simple programs using variables, operators, and control flow (if/else, loops).
Understand the concept of functions and modules.

learning_path.title: Working with Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Understand the purpose of an IDE.
Learn to use a popular IDE (e.g., VS Code, PyCharm, IntelliJ IDEA).
Configure the IDE for Python development.
Use the IDE for code editing, debugging, and running programs.
Understand basic IDE features like code completion and refactoring.

learning_path.title: Mobile Operating Systems: An Overview
objectives: Understand the architecture of mobile operating systems (Android, iOS).
Learn about the key components of a mobile OS (kernel, middleware, applications).
Understand the differences between Android and iOS.
Learn about mobile security and permissions.
Understand the mobile app development lifecycle.
skills: Knowledge of Android and iOS differences

learning_path.title: Introduction to Software Configuration Management (SCM) with Git
objectives: Understand the purpose of SCM.
Learn the basics of Git (version control system).
Create a Git repository.
Commit changes, branch, merge, and resolve conflicts.
Use a remote repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket).

learning_path.title: Introduction to the Internet of Things (IoT)
objectives: Understand the concept of IoT and its applications.
Learn about the components of an IoT system (sensors, actuators, connectivity, cloud).
Understand different IoT communication protocols (MQTT, CoAP).
Learn about IoT security considerations.
Explore popular IoT platforms (e.g., AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub).

learning_path.title: Data Migration Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the challenges of data migration.
Learn about different data migration strategies (big bang, trickle).
Understand data cleansing and transformation techniques.
Learn about data validation and verification.
Understand data migration planning and execution.

learning_path.title: Using Application-Specific Interfaces (APIs)
objectives: Understand the concept of APIs and their purpose.
Learn how to read API documentation.
Learn how to make API requests using Python (e.g., using the `requests` library).
Understand different API authentication methods (e.g., API keys, OAuth).
Learn how to handle API responses (JSON, XML).

learning_path.title: Developing Automated Data Migration Methods with Python
objectives: Learn how to automate data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) processes using Python.
Use Python libraries for data manipulation (e.g., Pandas).
Connect to different data sources (databases, files) using Python.
Implement error handling and logging in data migration scripts.
Schedule data migration tasks using Python.
skills: Connecting to data sources with Python

learning_path.title: Debugging Software with Python
objectives: Understand common types of software bugs.
Learn how to use the Python debugger (pdb).
Learn how to use logging for debugging.
Learn how to write unit tests to prevent bugs.
Understand debugging strategies and techniques.

learning_path.title: Software Design Patterns in Python
objectives: Understand the concept of software design patterns.
Learn about common creational patterns (e.g., Singleton, Factory).
Learn about common structural patterns (e.g., Adapter, Decorator).
Learn about common behavioral patterns (e.g., Observer, Strategy).
Apply design patterns to solve real-world programming problems.
